title: Fairly FAIR code in 10 steps: Initial Draft
author: Pieter Gijsbers
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
At the  2024 Open Science Retreat , we set out to create an institution-agnostic super-minimal guide that helps people publish code with their research. We wanted to achieve "Fairly FAIR" code: the guide helps ticks all of the FAIR boxes, except for those that pertain to code structure and quality. This allows for a really low entry level which already greatly enhances the reproducibility and reusability of the published work, and is possible to follow for anyone that's submitting a paper, even if no prior effort has been made to ensure FAIR code. We hope to incentivise people to take a first step, and learn more about FAIR code for future projects. 
 This release contains the main guide. We plan to follow up with a printable flyer and to convert the markdown into a simple website. 
 Credit goes to everyone that attended the open science retreat for their thoughtful input and suggestions, and in particular those that attended the FAIR code track.
